LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS THE OLYMPIC CLUB, PAUL JACQUES, GREG DEROSA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS MOOT AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FEHA AND THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE BANE ACT. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALLEGED ONLY AGAINST DOE 1. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS NEED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 2.112 AND SEGREGATE THE DISTINCT CLAIMS OF HARASSMENT, DISCRIMINATION, AND RETALIATION. PLAINTIFFS SHOULD ALSO PLEAD MORE FACTS REGARDING THE PROTECTED ACTIVITY THEY ALLEGEDLY ENGAGED IN AND THE ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS THEY ALLEGEDLY SUFFERED. PLAINTIFFS CANNOT PLEAD DISCRIMINATION AND RETALIATION CLAIMS AGAINST THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO PLEAD MORE FACTS TO SUPPORT THE CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

PLAINTIFFS HAVE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THAT PLAINTIFFS' AGES WERE A MOTIVATING REASON FOR DEFENDANTS TO ALTER PLAINTIFFS' WORK HOURS AND SHIFTS IN FAVOR OF YOUNGER EMPLOYEES. (SEE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT 48, 75.) DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HARASSMENT IS SUSTAINED WITH ONE LAST OPPORTUNITY FOR LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD, IF PLAINTIFFS CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT SHOW DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED HARASSMENT WAS MOTIVATED BY PLAINTIFFS' AGE, RACE, NATIONAL ORIGIN OR OTHER PROTECTED STATUS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Oct-31-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES PAUL GREEN, TEL. NO. 621-1501 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ANDREW M. WOLFE OF MONTY WHITE LLP, TEL. NO. 453-1010 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE OLYMPIC CLUB and GREG DEROSA'S MOTION FOR Summary Adjudication Of Issues is Granted. As an initial matter, Plaintiffs' response to the separate statement does not comply with CCP sec. 437c(b)(3) and CRC 3.1350(h). Although this is a ground for granting the motion, the Court also considered the merits of the motion, as discussed below. As to the fourth cause of action for discrimination as to Mr. Wong, Defendants met their initial burden by introducing evidence that Plaintiffs cannot establish that Mr. Wong suffered an adverse employment action because of his age or ethnicity. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 3, 66-67.) Plaintiffs have not raised triable issues of material fact. As to the fifth cause of action for retaliation as to Mr. Wong, Defendants met their initial burden by introducing evidence that Plaintiffs cannot establish that Mr. Wong suffered an adverse employment action or that any action occurred because of Mr. Wong's age or ethnicity. (UMF 3, 66-67.) Plaintiffs have not raised triable issues of material fact. As to the sixth cause of action for discrimination as to Ms. Canion, Defendants have met their initial burden by introducing evidence that Plaintiffs cannot establish that Ms. Canion suffered an adverse employment action because of her age or ethnicity. (UMF 107, 132, 137.) Plaintiffs have not raised a triable issue of material fact. As to the seventh cause of action for retaliation as to Ms. Canion, Defendants met their initial burden by introducing evidence that Plaintiffs cannot establish that Ms. Canion suffered an adverse employment action or that any action occurred because of Ms. Canion's age or ethnicity. (UMF 107, 125, 132, 137.) Plaintiffs have not raised a triable issue of material fact. As to the eighth cause of action for harassment against Defendant Greg DeRosa, Defendants met their initial burden by establishing that the complained of conduct was not severe or pervasive, nor was it motivated by Mr. Wong's age or race. (UMF 70-75.) Plaintiffs have not raised a triable issue of material fact. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Andrew M. Wolfe, Esq. of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley, Tel. 415.543.4800, for defendant and moving party;
James Paul Green, Esq. of the Law Office of James Paul Green, Tel. 415.621.1501, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE OLYMPIC CLUB, PAUL JACQUES and GREG DEROSA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for FEHA violations is sustained with leave to amend. Each plaintiff must comply with CRC 2.112 and segregate his/her distinct causes of action for discrimination and retaliation. Plaintiffs should also plead more facts regarding the adverse employment action. It is still unclear, as to each plaintiff, whether the Second Amended Complaint alleges that each plaintiff was terminated, constructively terminated, demoted, or whether some other adverse employment action occurred. Paragraph 43 sufficiently alleges that the plaintiffs engaged in protected activity to support the retaliation cause of action. The Court sustains the demurrer to the 5th cause of action for harassment with leave to amend. Plaintiffs shall more clearly allege that the harassment was motivated by animus pursuant to Gov't Code 12940(j). Defendants' request for judicial notice is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JAMES GREEN, ESQ., 415.621.1502, FOR PLAINIFF PAUL WONG.
JOHN DOOLING, ESQ., ROPERS MAJESKI KOHN BENTLEY, 415.543.4800, FOR DEFENDANTS THE OLYMPIC CLUB, PAUL JACQUES AND GREG DEROSA.

RULING: MATTER ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. DEFENDANTS THE OLYMPIC CLUB, PAUL JACQUES AND GREG DEROSA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained with leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action. As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiffs failed to allege what adverse employment action UNDER FEHA was taken against them to support an actionable claim of either retaliation or discrimination. As to the fifth cause of action, Plaintiffs failed to allege that any harassment they suffered was due to a characteristic of theirs protected by the FEHA. As to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiffs failed allege that any of their constitutional or statutory rights were interfered with by means of threats, intimidation or coercion. Because Plaintiffs were previously granted leave to amend to address the specified defects and their opposition papers do not suggest how they can cure these deficiencies. LEAVE TO AMEND GRANTED UP TO/INCLUDING NOVEMBER 1, 2012. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
